title: Wire the vendoring contract into CI — sync-core --check, core/** path filters, and ca-codex jobs are all absent
evidence: ADR-0011's stated answer to the v1 drift failure mode is 'CI enforces byte-identity between core/ and every vendored copy' and calls these jobs 'load-bearing and may never be made optional'. But `grep sync-core .github/` returns nothing; ci.yml's path filters (lines 22-29, 51-68) name only plugins/ca/**, plugins/ca-sandbox/**, and .github/** — neither `core/**` nor `plugins/ca-codex/**` appears anywhere in ci.yml or release.yml. A PR editing core/pysrc/ without re-running sync-core merges green: the `hooks` filter watches plugins/ca/hooks/** (unchanged), and no job runs `sync-core --check`. The decision log (decision-log.md:369) even claims 'CI path filters parameterized for a third plugin (core/** triggers both suites)' — not implemented.
impact: The three-copy layout's only anti-drift mechanism is a locally-run script nothing compels anyone to run. A core edit can ship with stale vendored copies (the two hosts silently enforcing different rules — exactly v1's death mode), and ca-codex has no version-bump guard, no hook-suite run, and no payload-staleness gate at all.
recommendation: Add a repo-wide CI job running `python tools/sync-core.py --check`; add `core/**` and `plugins/ca-codex/**` to the paths-filter so a core change triggers both plugins' suites (ADR-0011 point 3); clone the version-bump guard for ca-codex's independent versioning; add the new jobs to ci-passed's needs list.
acceptance_criteria: A PR that edits core/pysrc/*.py without re-vendoring fails CI
A core/** -only diff runs the hook test suites
ca-codex payload change on a published ca-codex version fails the bump gate
